title: Odontoglossum peruvianum
taxonomy: Odontoglossum peruvianum is a species of orchid, classified within the family Orchidaceae. This classification places it in the subfamily Orchidoideae. Further, it is placed in the tribe Maxillarieae. The genus Odontoglossum is a part of the subtribe Maxillariinae. It is native to the tropical Andes of South America.

intro: Odontoglossum peruvianum, also known as the Peruvian orchid, is a species of orchid native to the Andean region of South America. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties, which may help to alleviate respiratory issues, skin conditions, and wounds. The bioactive constituents of Odontoglossum peruvianum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as skin conditions, such as eczema and acne.
